  "findings": [
    "Thermal growth offset was ignored during shaft alignment on hot service equipment.",
    "Operating alignment is out of tolerance due to thermal expansion, causing high vibration and stress on bearings and seals."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down the equipment immediately to prevent catastrophic bearing or coupling failure.",
    "Perform hot alignment checks or calculate thermal growth offsets using manufacturer data and API 686 guidelines.",
    "Re-align the machine cold with the correct target offsets applied."
  ],
  "rationale": "Aligning hot service equipment without accounting for thermal growth offsets violates API 686 standards. Once the machinery reaches operating temperature, the shafts will expand and move out of alignment, leading to rapid degradation of bearings, couplings, and mechanical seals.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 686 Chapter 4 (Machinery Alignment)",
    "Laser Alignment System Manuals (Thermal Growth Compensation)"
  ]
}
